title: Subuidname running as unexpected UID
body:
### Issue Description

I have been testing podman in rootless mode with different ways of specifying namespaces. I used `--subuidname` as an option with options as described [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/21147) and ran into strange behaviour. 

I will say that this may be me misunderstanding or a user error and in that case if you could explain that'd be very useful :)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Edit `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` to contain overlapping uid ranges as described [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/21147). Mine is 
```
river:524288:65536
river:10000:65536
test:11000:1024
```
2. run `podman run -d --rm --name test-container --subuidname test ubi9 sleep 100`
3. run `podman top test-container user huser`

### Describe the results you received

The output of the top command was:
```
USER        HUSER
root        20999
```

So the root user in the container is operation as 20999 on the system. Through changing the values under `/etc/subuid` it became clear that the value in  `/etc/subuid` was being treated as a intermediate uid in the user namespace. So it was the same as using `--uidmap=0:11000:1024` (it is definitely using the full range as tested through using the `--user` flag.

### Describe the results you expected

The output I would expect from the top command would be:
```
USER        HUSER
root          11000
```

As the mapping was defined in `/etc/subuid` I would have expected it to be treated as a uid in the initial namespace.

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

comments.body: I've had a dig around in the code on github and I'm now certain this is unintended behaviour. In [ParseIDMapping](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/dc8d2c13fd0044cd0cd2117f780e1a5be96238ee/pkg/util/utils.go#L954) the section that deals with subuidname is [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/dc8d2c13fd0044cd0cd2117f780e1a5be96238ee/pkg/util/utils.go#L991). It can be clearly seen that it is avoiding the path that would correct the [U/G]ID to refer to one in the parent namespace. I'm unsure if the bug is coming from idtools or not - I unfortunately have no experience with go but will try and dig deeper if I get the time.

I could very well be missing something, but I think `--subuidname` never guarantees that exact range, but rather adds to the existing pool of uid mappings that podman can use. The allocation then happens across the collective pool of UIDs available to the intermediate namespace, which includes the UIDs for the rootless user running the container.

I think the [docs](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/95230cb315d7c1b508460d36bac39ad6c65e5e08/docs/source/markdown/options/subuidname.md#L4) say that it should run in that specific user namespace? If they are not then I would say that the docs should be updated to make that more clear.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
